body.title: [sprint] Teach-on-miss: LLM pushes missing nodes (with checkable target) under a proof-on-write gate
body.intention: I intend that on a miss the LLM grows the graph (never answers), so every answer stays graph-native and the graph permanently improves.
body.description:
**Problem:** THE KEYSTONE. On a miss the system must NOT ask the LLM for an answer (wrong shape) — the LLM must push the missing nodes so the graph produces the answer itself.
**Proposed shape:** (1) miss-detection: text activates too few nodes / below confidence, or LIKE returns nothing plausible — define the threshold (graph-tree judgment). (2) On miss, invoke the LLM (free Ollama Cloud / Hex) to push missing concept-nodes (+edges) WITH a checkable target ('after these, this text should land near X,Y'). (3) PROOF-ON-WRITE GATE: commit the pushed nodes ONLY IF the graph then actually produces the stated target; else reject. (4) Provenance-tag pushed nodes as LLM-sourced. teach-on-miss SUPERSEDES any LLM-compare-for-training path.
**OPEN DESIGN:** miss threshold; the target-assertion format; the verify step; rollback of rejected pushes.
**Affected files:** the embedder/teach loop; word_graph write path; provenance.
**Design rules:** Inference Proxy only for the LLM call; proof-on-write mirrors proof-on-close (CP1 — a bad node is permanent, gate it); log every node write.
**Scope boundary:** miss-detect + teach + proof-on-write gate + provenance; node-merge is a sibling ticket.
**Completion criteria:** a forced miss triggers an LLM node-push; with a satisfiable target the nodes commit and the graph then produces the target; with an UNsatisfiable/bad target the push is REJECTED (not committed). Test proves both accept and reject paths.
**Test plan:** integration: planted miss + good target -> nodes committed + graph produces target; planted miss + bad target -> rejected, graph unchanged.
